description: Repo-local dogfood receipts for real Lane 1 repair-loop attempts, including successful, resolved, unchanged, and missing-receipt outcomes.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: readiness and attempt-ledger report assertions over repair_kind attempted/improved/unchanged/regressed counts
cases.attempted_repair: Added repair-route quality rollups and top failing/missing evidence route fields to attempt-ledger/readiness outputs.
cases.evidence_movement: Readiness can now show repair_kind success-rate evidence instead of only packet counts.
cases.operator_note: The route-quality attempt improved analyzer-backlog signal without changing badge, LSP, PR, or CI authority.
must_not_change: public badge semantics
CI gate defaults
autonomous repair behavior
cases.reason: A real merged repair-loop PR converted attempt outcomes into route-quality evidence.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: route-quality backlog assertions that non_claims preserve the actionability boundary and keep metrics from promoting or downgrading repair packets
cases.attempted_repair: Added the route-quality backlog non-claim that route-quality evidence alone must not promote or downgrade actionability.
cases.evidence_movement: The route-quality backlog now carries an explicit actionability-boundary non-claim while keeping repair-route metrics separate from public repair packet eligibility.
cases.operator_note: This records route-learning safety progress. It improves analyzer backlog guidance without changing public packet eligibility, badge semantics, CI gates, or autonomous repair behavior.
must_not_change: public repair packet actionability
public badge semantics
CI gate defaults
provider integration
mutation execution
cases.reason: A real merged route-quality PR made the analyzer backlog actionability boundary explicit so outcome metrics cannot be mistaken for delegation authority.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: readiness and swarm-plan assertions that packets excluded for missing_repair_kind route to missing-context remediation instead of public repair queues
cases.attempted_repair: Routed missing_repair_kind projection exclusions into the blocked-state/missing-context path so readiness names the field that must be supplied before a packet can be repair-ready.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: missing repair-kind blockers now carry a named blocked-state route, example packet identity, and next action instead of being ambiguous public projection exclusions.
cases.operator_note: This improves Lane 1 repair routing clarity without making an incomplete packet actionable and without changing public badge, CI, LSP, PR, or agent authority.
must_not_change: do not emit repair packets without repair_kind
do not count missing-repair-kind blockers as public actionable gaps
do not change badge semantics
do not change CI gate defaults
do not add provider calls or autonomous edits
cases.reason: A real merged readiness PR improved the trust loop by turning an incomplete repair packet into a named blocked route with a concrete remediation field instead of overclaiming actionability.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: readiness top_limitation_routes assertions that legacy limitation backlog packets fill standard non-claims, why_not_actionable, unlock_condition, and explicit unknown evidence class values
cases.attempted_repair: Hardened readiness projection for older or partial limitation backlog packets so top limitation routes preserve analyzer-route guidance instead of emitting empty or repair-looking rows.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: legacy limitation backlog routes now receive standard non-claims, fallback non-actionability text, fallback unlock conditions, and explicit unknown evidence-class values while staying non-actionable.
cases.operator_note: This dogfoods negative-capability routing. The improvement makes analyzer backlog safer to inspect, but it deliberately does not create public repair packets or swarm-ready work.
must_not_change: do not mark limitation backlog packets public_projection_eligible
do not mark limitation backlog packets swarm-ready
do not emit verify or receipt commands for limitation backlog items
do not change public badge semantics
do not change CI gate defaults
do not add provider calls or mutation execution
cases.reason: A real merged limitation-backlog PR made non-actionable analyzer routes operational for legacy inputs without crossing the repair-delegation boundary.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: readiness JSON and Markdown assertions that top_next_action equals next_actions[0]
cases.attempted_repair: Added a stable top_next_action projection for downstream surfaces to consume directly.
cases.evidence_movement: The missing readiness projection gap was resolved by preserving next_actions[0] as a named contract field.
cases.operator_note: This makes LSP/PR/CI/badge projection thinner because they do not need to reinterpret raw findings for the first useful action.
must_not_change: next_actions ranking source
raw finding interpretation
default advisory posture
cases.reason: A real merged readiness PR closed the missing top-next-action projection gap.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: dogfood fixture proving one receipt-backed packet preserves canonical identity through attempt-ledger and readiness
cases.attempted_repair: Added a surface-projection alignment dogfood corpus and dogfood report section.
cases.evidence_movement: The packet identity, repair kind, verify command, receipt command/state, outcome, and readiness top_next_action are now pinned together.
cases.operator_note: This improved proof that downstream surfaces are thin consumers of canonical state, but it deliberately did not change those surfaces.
must_not_change: badge endpoint behavior
LSP/editor UX behavior
PR comment publishing
CI gate behavior
cases.reason: A real merged dogfood PR added receipt-backed projection alignment proof.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: targeted-test outcome fixture with unchanged before/after grip evidence
cases.attempted_repair: Exercised the outcome join where targeted movement exists but the static evidence did not improve.
cases.evidence_movement: Outcome reporting kept the unchanged attempt visible and did not mark the gap resolved.
cases.operator_note: This is intentionally not a success case; it proves failed or insufficient attempts do not disappear.
must_not_change: resolved classification
receipt-missing visibility
orphan receipt handling
cases.reason: A real dogfood outcome pass recorded unchanged evidence as visible non-success.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: packet guidance for add_exact_error_variant that names the concrete error variant and rejects broad Err/is_err/expect_err evidence
cases.attempted_repair: Sharpened add_exact_error_variant handoff guidance so the packet tells agents to extract the error, match the exact variant or payload, and avoid broad error-only checks.
cases.evidence_movement: The failed unchanged add_exact_error_variant route now has a receipt-backed guidance improvement attempt without reclassifying the original unchanged repair as successful.
cases.operator_note: This records route-learning progress. It does not hide the unchanged repair attempt or claim the original gap was resolved.
must_not_change: unchanged attempt visibility
public badge semantics
CI gate defaults
provider integration
mutation execution
cases.reason: A real merged route-quality PR improved add_exact_error_variant repair guidance after an unchanged dogfood attempt showed broad error guidance was not trustworthy enough.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: exact error-variant observer guidance that names the concrete variant and rejects broad Err/is_err/expect_err evidence
cases.attempted_repair: Re-ran the add_exact_error_variant route through the bounded exact-error-variant guidance tests instead of treating the original unchanged targeted outcome as the latest route result.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: the add_exact_error_variant route now has a focused verify command and durable dogfood receipt while the historical unchanged attempt remains visible.
cases.operator_note: This records route-learning progress for the failed exact-error-variant route; it does not claim the original static gap was resolved or change public badge or gate semantics.
must_not_change: unchanged attempt visibility
resolved classification
public badge semantics
CI gate defaults
provider integration
mutation execution
cases.reason: A real follow-up run proved the add_exact_error_variant route can preserve a focused verify command and durable receipt path without turning the historical unchanged attempt into success.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: report golden observer for rendered configuration label
cases.attempted_repair: Dry-ran a ready packet and checked verify/receipt command separation without fabricating missing receipts.
cases.evidence_movement: The verify command passed, while the receipt command named required missing receipt files.
cases.operator_note: This records a non-closed loop explicitly; a missing receipt remains a blocker rather than hidden success.
must_not_change: receipt fabrication
source files
generated tests
provider calls
cases.missing_receipt_reason: receipt command reported required local receipts were absent
cases.reason: A real dogfood dry-run pass proved missing receipts stay named and do not become implicit success.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: report golden observer for rendered configuration label with a durable dogfood receipt
cases.attempted_repair: Recorded the report-config-label output observer route with the same bounded golden verification and a durable dogfood receipt path.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: the add_output_observer route now has a receipt-backed follow-up instead of only an attempted_no_receipt dry run.
cases.operator_note: The original missing-receipt dry run stays in the corpus as historical evidence; this follow-up only proves the route can be receipted, not that public badge or gate semantics changed.
must_not_change: receipt fabrication
source files
public badge semantics
CI gate defaults
provider integration
mutation execution
cases.reason: A real follow-up run proved the add_output_observer route can preserve a runnable verify command and durable receipt path without turning the historical missing-receipt attempt into success.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: fixture assertions that local/computed boundary operands remain named static limitations and do not emit add_boundary_assertion packets
cases.attempted_repair: Pinned local/computed boundary operand cases in evidence-quality and swarm-plan corpora so the analyzer backlog route stays precise and non-actionable.
cases.evidence_movement: The local/computed activation boundary route now has fixture-backed proof that RIPR names the limitation instead of inventing candidate values or public repair packets.
cases.operator_note: This is a successful negative-capability repair-loop attempt: it improved route trust by making unsafe actionability stay blocked.
must_not_change: public projection eligibility for static limitations
add_boundary_assertion promotion
candidate value inference
production behavior
cases.reason: A real merged limitation-route PR made local/computed boundary operands more trustworthy by preserving named non-actionability instead of routing unsafe repair work.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: fixture-backed direct owner call where predicate operands are parameter field projections and the related test passes same-test struct values with literal fields
cases.attempted_repair: Taught the boundary activation path to resolve simple parameter field operands, such as left.value == right.value, through same-test struct field bindings when direct owner-call arguments are visible.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: a fixture-backed predicate boundary can now observe concrete activation values and clear the equality-boundary missing discriminator when both parameter field projections resolve to equal literal fields.
cases.operator_note: This is an analyzer-route dogfood slice. Opaque field projections, computed locals, and iterator-derived operands remain blocked instead of being promoted to public repair packets.
must_not_change: do not invent values for opaque struct arguments
do not resolve arbitrary computed locals
do not resolve iterator-derived boundary operands
do not emit public repair packets from static-limitation backlog
do not change gate or badge semantics
cases.reason: A local analyzer slice narrowed the local/computed boundary backlog by resolving safe parameter field projections while keeping unsupported computed and iterator cases non-actionable.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: fixture-backed predicate boundary where the unresolved operand is bound by a closure parameter, such as function.id == owner
cases.attempted_repair: Split closure-derived boundary operands out of the generic local/computed boundary limitation route while preserving non-actionable status.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: closure-local boundary operands now route to analysis/closure-boundary-operand-resolution instead of blending with local/computed or iterator-derived boundary work.
cases.operator_note: This is analyzer-route dogfood. It improves work allocation only; it does not emit a public repair packet, infer candidate values, or move badge/gate counters.
must_not_change: do not invent values for closure parameters
do not route closure operands as iterator-derived operands
do not emit public repair packets from static-limitation backlog
do not change gate or badge semantics
cases.reason: A local analyzer slice made closure-derived boundary operands a precise static-limitation route while keeping candidate-value inference blocked.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: same-file call-presence limitation where the unresolved owner call is a method-chain expression such as description.clone()
cases.attempted_repair: Split same-file method-chain call-presence owner-call limitations into a dedicated repair route while preserving non-actionable status.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: method-chain same-file owner-call limitations now route to analysis/same-file-method-chain-owner-call-tracing instead of blending with the generic same-file owner-call route.
cases.operator_note: This is analyzer-route dogfood. It improves work allocation only; it does not prove owner-call reachability, emit public repair packets, or move badge/gate counters.
must_not_change: do not treat same-file proximity alone as owner-call activation
do not promote method-chain limitations to public repair packets
do not invent observed values for call-presence arguments
do not change gate or badge semantics
cases.reason: A local analyzer slice made same-file method-chain owner-call limitations a precise route while keeping the remaining same-file backlog non-actionable.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: evidence-record and benchmark fixture assertions that call-presence target-token affinity remains a named static limitation and does not satisfy activation
cases.attempted_repair: Split call-presence target-token affinity out of the broader assertion-target affinity owner-call limitation route while preserving non-actionable status.
cases.evidence_movement: The top sampled limitation route now points to a precise call-presence analyzer backlog item instead of blending target-token affinity with other assertion-target affinity.
cases.operator_note: This is a successful negative-capability repair-loop attempt: it improved analyzer backlog precision without emitting a public repair packet or treating target-token assertions as owner-call activation.
must_not_change: public projection eligibility for static limitations
activation satisfied by call-target assertion text
direct owner-call or helper-call requirements
public badge semantics
cases.reason: A real merged limitation-route PR made the highest-volume sampled call-presence target-affinity limitation more trustworthy by routing it to a precise analyzer backlog item while keeping it non-actionable.

cases.target_test_or_observer_shape: fixture-backed test-local one-hop helper where an Err(...) wrapper directly contains the production owner call for value-insensitive call-presence activation
cases.attempted_repair: Taught assertion-target affinity owner-call tracing to recognize a bounded test-local helper that wraps the production owner call in `Err(...)`, while keeping arbitrary Result wrappers and affinity-only evidence limited.
cases.evidence_movement: improved: the analyzer route has fixture and benchmark proof that Err-wrapped helper owner calls can satisfy value-insensitive activation without inventing observed values or promoting assertion-target affinity alone to public repair work.
cases.operator_note: This is analyzer-route dogfood for an already-merged fixture-backed route. It records movement without changing actionability, gates, badges, or the remaining assertion-target affinity backlog.
must_not_change: do not treat arbitrary Result wrappers as multi-hop helper tracing
do not treat assertion-target affinity alone as activation without a helper or direct owner call
do not invent observed values for call-presence arguments
do not apply this route to predicate-boundary value checks
do not emit public repair packets from static-limitation backlog
do not change gate or badge semantics
cases.reason: A real merged analyzer slice added bounded Err-wrapped helper proof for assertion-target affinity owner-call tracing while preserving affinity-only and non-actionable backlog boundaries.

cases.operator_note: This is a successful negative-capability dogfood attempt. It made the top analyzer backlog route operational without promoting same-file proximity into repair work.
must_not_change: do not treat same-file proximity as owner-call activation
do not emit public repair packets from static-limitation backlog
do not make limitation backlog packets swarm-ready
do not invent exact candidate values
do not change public badge or CI semantics
cases.reason: A real merged limitation-backlog PR turned the highest-volume sampled same-file owner-call route into inspectable analyzer backlog evidence while keeping the route non-actionable.
